evals.name: raw-search-smoke
evals.prompt: Use Synthetic Search to run a query for rust async await and show me the exact curl request.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the raw search endpoint, uses POST https://api.synthetic.new/v2/search with the Authorization bearer header and JSON body, and points to references/api.md.

assertions.text: Mentions POST https://api.synthetic.new/v2/search with a JSON body containing query
Routes to references/api.md for the exact request shape

evals.name: jq-urls-smoke
evals.prompt: Use Synthetic Search, but I only need a URL list I can pipe into another script.
evals.expected_output: Uses curl plus jq -r '.results[].url' and routes to the patterns guidance rather than the readable helper output.

assertions.text: Uses jq to extract .results[].url
Routes to references/patterns.md

evals.name: helper-script-readable-output
evals.prompt: I have SYNTHETIC_API_KEY set. Give me the easiest command for readable terminal output instead of raw JSON.
evals.expected_output: Uses node scripts/search.js with a plain query, optionally with --content or --limit, and treats the helper as the best human-readable path.

assertions.text: Uses node scripts/search.js for readable output
Does not force raw curl output when a readable helper is requested

evals.name: quota-check-smoke
evals.prompt: Check my Synthetic limits before I burn through requests. What endpoint or helper should I use?
evals.expected_output: Uses GET /v2/quotas or node scripts/search.js quotas, notes that /quotas does not count against subscription limits per the docs, and routes to references/api.md.

assertions.text: Mentions GET https://api.synthetic.new/v2/quotas or the quotas helper command
Notes that /quotas is documented as not counting against subscription limits

evals.name: published-mismatch-edge-case
evals.prompt: The docs say Synthetic Search returns a published timestamp, but my live JSON doesn't have it. Which one should I trust?
evals.expected_output: Explains that published is documented but was absent in live testing, recommends treating it as optional, and routes to references/gotchas.md.

assertions.text: Treats published as optional and prefers live behavior over stale examples
Routes to references/gotchas.md

evals.name: empty-query-edge-case
evals.prompt: Synthetic Search is returning a 500 for my request, and the only thing in the body is query: empty string. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Explains that empty query currently returns a blank-body 500, recommends validating non-empty query strings client-side, and points to references/gotchas.md.

assertions.text: Explains the empty-query 500 behavior and recommends local validation
Routes to references/gotchas.md

evals.name: missing-query-disclosure
evals.prompt: My POST body was malformed and Synthetic says query is missing. Show me the correct request and where the exact schema lives.
evals.expected_output: Provides the proper JSON body with query, mentions the 400 missing-query behavior, and routes to references/api.md.

assertions.text: Shows a JSON body containing query
Routes to references/api.md for the request shape

evals.name: structured-probe-disclosure
evals.prompt: I want a machine-readable verification run for Synthetic Search, including auth errors and quotas, not a hand-written note.
evals.expected_output: Uses python3 scripts/probe_synthetic_search.py --mode all and explains that it returns structured JSON covering search, quotas, and error cases.

assertions.text: Uses the probe script instead of only ad hoc shell commands
Mentions that the probe covers search, quotas, and errors

evals.name: configuration-routing
evals.prompt: I want to use Synthetic Search but I have not set anything up yet. What do I need installed and configured first?
evals.expected_output: Routes to references/configuration.md, requires SYNTHETIC_API_KEY, and notes curl, optional jq, Node, and Python prerequisites.

assertions.text: Routes to references/configuration.md
Mentions SYNTHETIC_API_KEY as required

evals.name: negative-other-search-provider
evals.prompt: Use Tavily to search for recent AI startup funding rounds.
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger synthetic-search because the user explicitly asked for Tavily, not Synthetic.

assertions.text: Does not route to synthetic-search when another search provider is explicitly requested

evals.name: negative-browser-automation
evals.prompt: Open this site, click around the navigation, and capture a screenshot of the pricing tab.
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger synthetic-search because the task is browser automation, not Synthetic API search.

assertions.text: Does not route to synthetic-search for browser automation tasks

evals.name: display-limit-edge-case
evals.prompt: Use the helper to show just the top three Synthetic Search results, but tell me whether the API itself was limited or only the display.
evals.expected_output: Uses node scripts/search.js --limit 3 but explains that the display is trimmed locally and the API still returns its full result set.

assertions.text: Explains that --limit is display-only
Uses the helper script for the human-readable case
